summary: The scoped test and quality claims reproduce, but resume_from accepts checkpoints belonging to different runs, so an interrupted run can treat another run's stages as completed. The checkpoint/repeated-interruption acceptance claim is therefore not established.

reproduced.claim: ruff format/check on scoped implementation and tests
programming no-excuse audit on five production files

findings.title: Resume accepts a checkpoint prefix assembled from different runs
findings.repro: Construct ordinal 0 as Checkpoint(run_key='run-A', stage='01', ...) and ordinal 1 as Checkpoint(run_key='run-B', stage='02', ...), then call resume_from(('01','02','03'), checkpoints). It returns ResumePlan(next_stage='03', completed_stages=('01','02')) instead of CheckpointSequenceError.
findings.impact: Stale or incorrectly queried persisted state can skip work in the wrong run, defeating safe checkpoint resume and repeated-interruption guarantees.

findings.title: Input hashing accepts non-standard JSON numeric constants
findings.repro: deterministic_input_hash('NaN'), deterministic_input_hash('Infinity'), and deterministic_input_hash('-Infinity') all return hashes.
findings.impact: The function claims canonical JSON hashing but accepts and re-emits values outside interoperable RFC JSON, allowing boundary behavior to vary across producers/parsers.

findings.title: Malformed HTTP status values can enter retry classification
findings.repro: HttpFailureError(status_code=700) is retried three times and ends as RetryExhaustedError because every status >=500 is treated as transient.
findings.impact: An invalid adapter value consumes the retry budget instead of failing validation; valid validation/auth/hard-risk and non-transient 4xx exclusions behave correctly.

findings.title: Repository-wide basedpyright gate is currently red outside Todo 3 scope
findings.repro: uv run basedpyright reports dict[str, SecretStr] incompatible with the HTTP client's headers parameter.
findings.impact: Todo 3 scoped typing passes, but the evidence's unqualified basedpyright success statement is stale under the current dirty worktree.

adversarial_checks.transition_graphs: All enum variants are graph keys; exhaustive runtime probe observed exactly 10 allowed/39 rejected run pairs and 13 allowed/51 rejected stage pairs.
adversarial_checks.retry_classifier: Validation, authentication, hard-risk, and valid non-transient 4xx make one attempt; 408/425/429 and 5xx use bounded injected delays; no real retry sleeps are used in tests.
adversarial_checks.clock: Lifecycle transition clock is injected; wall clock remains only as the production default.
adversarial_checks.checkpoint: Ordinal/stage contiguity is checked, but checkpoint run identity is not.
adversarial_checks.dirty_worktree: Parent repository reports app/ as untracked, so historical diffs cannot establish changed-file provenance; no product files were edited by this verifier.
adversarial_checks.flaky_timing: 48-test run completed in 0.08s; timeout test uses AnyIO deadline and retry delays use a recorder.
adversarial_checks.misleading_output: Scoped gates pass; repository-wide basedpyright does not.
